messages.content:
You are a GDPR compliance analyst. When given a document excerpt, evaluate it step by step against each GDPR requirement. For each step, quote the relevant text if found, or state it is missing. End with a structured verdict listing all violations. Always follow this exact format:

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): ...
Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): ...
Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): ...
Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): ...
Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): ...
Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): ...
Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): ...
Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): ...
Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): ...
Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): ...

VERDICT: [Compliant / Non-Compliant / Partially Compliant]
VIOLATIONS: [list each article breached]
RECOMMENDATIONS: [one line per fix needed]

Assess the following job board platform privacy policy for GDPR compliance:

"JobPortal collects your name, CV, work history, education, and contact details when you create a profile. Your profile is visible to registered employers searching for candidates. We use your data to match you with relevant job listings. You can set your profile to private at any time. Data is stored on servers in the US. We retain inactive profiles for 2 years before deletion."

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): [FAIL] No lawful basis stated. Contract (Art. 6(1)(b)) or legitimate interest (Art. 6(1)(f)) is implied for matching services — neither is named.

Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): [PARTIAL] CVs frequently contain disability, health, or nationality information. No acknowledgement or handling procedure stated.

Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): [PARTIAL] Job matching and employer profile visibility are stated. Whether profile data is used for analytics, marketing, or sold to third parties is not addressed.

Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): [PASS] Data collected is proportionate for a job board and candidate matching service.

Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): [PASS] 2-year retention for inactive profiles is clearly stated.

Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): [PARTIAL] Only profile visibility control (partial restriction) is referenced. No formal rights listed.

Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): [PARTIAL] Employers are named as recipients. No disclosure of technical platform processors.

Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): [FAIL] US servers disclosed but no transfer safeguard mechanism stated.

Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): [FAIL] Not addressed.

Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): [FAIL] Not addressed.

VERDICT: Non-Compliant
VIOLATIONS: Art. 6 (no lawful basis), Art. 9 (CV special category risk not addressed), Art. 5 (purpose scope unclear), Art. 15-21 (no formal rights), Art. 28 (platform processors not disclosed), Art. 46 (US transfer safeguards absent), Art. 37 (DPO not addressed), Art. 33 (no breach notification)
RECOMMENDATIONS:
- State lawful basis for each processing activity
- Add a note advising candidates not to include special category data in CVs and state how it will be handled
- Clarify whether profile data is used for any purposes beyond job matching
- Add a full data subject rights section
- Disclose platform infrastructure processors
- Reference US transfer safeguards (SCCs)
- Assess and appoint DPO
- Add breach notification commitment
